app.name: Industrial Ethernet
app.description:
U-Phy Device Application
This application shows how to implement Industrial Ethernet connectivity using the U-Phy middleware. Currently Profinet, Ethernet/IP, CC-Link IE Field Basic and Modbus TCP are supported.

The example implements a basic I/O device connecting inputs and outputs to to the EVK buttons and LEDs. Profinet GSDML, EtherNet/IP EDS and CC-Link CSP+ files are found in the project folder.

After building and programming the example, the EVK can be connected to a Profinet, Ethernet/IP, CCIEFB or Modbus TCP network for evaluation.

RT-Labs U-Phy middleware enables Industrial Ethernet connectivity for Infineon microcontrollers. Key features:
  • Profinet, EtherNet/IP, CC-Link IE Field Basic (CCIEFB) and Modbus TCP support
  • Define process data and device characteristics using U-Phy Device Builder
  • Build time generation of GSDML, EDS, CSP+ and device specific code
  • U-Phy Middleware - U-Phy MTB Middleware on Github
  • U-Phy - General introduction to U-Phy concept and features

app.name: EtherCAT master stack
app.description:
SOEM Master Example Application
This application demonstrates how to implement Industrial Ethernet connectivity using the SOEM middleware.

It includes reference examples for generic use as well as a dedicated example for operating towards an Infineon XMC4800 slave board running a demo image (supplied).

RT-Labs SOEM Middleware enables Industrial Ethernet connectivity for Infineon microcontrollers using EtherCAT. Key features include:
  • EtherCAT master for cyclic I/O and motion devices
  • Lightweight, portable C code
  • Bus scan, topology validation, and SII (slave EEPROM) access
  • CoE support (SDO/OD access) and PDO mapping
  • Distributed Clocks (DC) synchronization for precise cycle timing
  • Simple API for state transitions and process data exchange
  • Reference examples and diagnostics utilities
  • SOEM Example SOEM Example Application on GitHub
  • SOEM Middleware SOEM Middleware on GitHub<
  • SOEM General introduction to SOEM concepts and features

app.name: CANopen stack
app.description:
CANopen Example Application
This example demonstrates how to enable CANopen connectivity using the C-Open middleware.

RT-Labs C-Open Middleware enables CANopen connectivity for Infineon microcontrollers. Key features include:
  • Dual Role Support: Implement both CANopen master and slave devices using the same stack
  • Network Management (NMT): Full support for network initialization, configuration, and state management
  • Service Data Objects (SDO): Expedited and segmented transfers for configuration and parameter access
  • Process Data Objects (PDO): High-performance real-time data exchange with configurable mapping
  • Emergency Objects (EMCY): Error reporting and diagnostic capabilities
  • Heartbeat and Node Guarding: Network monitoring and fault detection mechanisms
  • Layer Setting Services (LSS): Dynamic node-ID assignment and bitrate configuration (CiA 305)
  • Multi-Instance Support: Run multiple CANopen networks simultaneously
  • Portable Architecture: Written to a OS abstraction layer
  • Minimal Footprint: Optimized for resource-constrained embedded systems
  • Production Ready: Tested with CANopen Conformance Test Tool
